perturbation.original_text: Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week.
perturbation.changed_text: Galacticomm will make reasonable efforts to provide a person(s) to address technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) during standard business hours.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'must provide' to 'will make reasonable efforts' and limiting the availability to 'standard business hours', the obligation to provide 24/7 support is weakened. This contradicts the implied guarantee of continuous service availability, as now Galaticomm can argue that 24/7 response is not a guaranteed promise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Galacticomm will make reasonable efforts to provide a person(s) to address technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) during standard business hours. This person(s) must be available by beeper or telephone. Horst Entertainment shall provide this same 24 hour service at the broadcast location.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 24/7 support, while the changed text limits support to standard business hours. The contradicted text mentions 24 hour service. This creates a direct contradiction because the modified clause weakens the original 24/7 obligation, making it unclear whether Galacticomm is obligated to provide continuous support as implied elsewhere in the agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction,' because it is 'making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement for cause, Horst Entertainment Inc. may be offered the opportunity to purchase a license copy of the software at a price to be determined.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the option for Horst Entertainment to purchase a license contingent on the reason for termination ('for cause') and introduces uncertainty regarding the price ('at a price to be determined'). It goes against the original terms that state they 'will have the right to purchase' and fixes the amount.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement for cause, Horst Entertainment Inc. may be offered the opportunity to purchase a license copy of the software at a price to be determined. All parties have read and fully agree to all terms and conditions as set forth in this Web Hosting Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Horst Entertainment Inc. 'will have the right to purchase' a software license for a fixed amount ($15,000). The changed text makes this right contingent on the reason for termination ('for cause') and introduces uncertainty ('at a price to be determined'). This directly contradicts the original, unconditional right and the fixed price, creating a strong inconsistency within the document. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because key obligations, such as the right to purchase and the price, are defined inconsistently.
